registers.name: Mode
registers.description: Indicates the operational state of the port. The PD controller has limited functionality in some modes. Table 3-3. Mode Register Field Descriptions

fields.name: Mode
fields.description: The mode described in 4 ASCII characters. 'APP ' indicates that the PD controller is fully functioning in the application firmware where all registers are available. 'BOOT' indicates that the PD controller is booting in dead battery. 'PTCH' indicates that the PD controller is in patch mode. Any other values indicates that the PD controller is functioning in limited capacity. In 'BOOT' and 'PTCH' only the follow register addresses are accessible, Mode, Command, Data, Int Event,  Int Mask, Int Clear, and Boot Flags.

registers.name: Customer Use
registers.description: These 8 bytes are allocated for customer use as needed. The PD controller does not use this register. This register may be changed during application customization. Table 3-4. Customer Use Register Field Descriptions

fields.name: Customer Use
fields.description: These 8 bytes are allocated for customer use as needed. The PD controller does not use this register. This register may be changed during application customization.

registers.name: Command Register for I2C1
registers.description: Command register for the primary command interface. Cleared to 0x0000_0000 by the PD Controller during initialization and after successful processing of every command. If an unrecognized command is written to this register, it is replaced by a 4CC value of '!CMD'. Table 3-5. Command Register for I2C1 Field Descriptions

fields.name: Command
fields.description: Command register for the primary command interface. Cleared to 0x0000_0000 by the PD Controller during initialization and after successful processing of every command. If an unrecognized command is written to this register, it is replaced by a 4CC value of  '!CMD'.

registers.name: Data Register for CMD1
registers.description: Data register for the primary command interface (CMD1). Table 3-6. Data Register for CMD1 Field Descriptions

fields.name: Data
fields.description: Data register for the primary command interface (CMD1).

registers.name: Interrupt Event for I2C1
registers.description: Interrupt event bit field for IRQ. If any bit in this register is 1, then the IRQ pin is pulled low. Bytes 1 to 10 of this register are port-specific, but Byte 11(

fields.name: I2C Controller NACked
fields.description: A transaction on the I2C Controller was NACKed.

fields.name: Ready for Patch
fields.description: Device ready for a patch bundle from the host.

fields.name: Patch Loaded
fields.description: Patch was loaded to the device.

fields.name: MBRD Buffer Ready
fields.description: Receive memory buffer full and ready to be read using the 'MBRd'  command.

fields.name: Liquid Detection
fields.description: Asserted when Liquid Detection is detected or removed. Read 0x98 to determine the state of Liquid Detection.

fields.name: Ext DCDC Source Safe State
fields.description: Used for EC controlled battery charger or DC/DC applications to indicate when the PD controller is no longer going to act as a source. This interrupt will be set when acting as a source and receiving/ sending an Accept message to a Power Role Swap.

fields.name: Ext DCDC Sink Safe State
fields.description: Used for EC controlled battery charger or DC/DC applications to indicate when the PD controller is no longer going to act as a sin. This interrupt will be set when acting as a sink and receiving/sending an Accept message to a Power Role Swap. This interrupt will also be set when acting as a sink and receiving an Explicit PD Contract Accept from the connected source.

fields.name: Unable to Source Error
fields.description: The Source was unable to increase the voltage to the negotiated voltage of the contract.

fields.name: Plug Early Notification
fields.description: A connection has been detected but not debounced.

fields.name: Sink Transition Completed
fields.description: This event only occurs when in source mode  (PD_STATUS.PresentPDRole = 1b). It occurs tSrcTransition (ms)  after sending an Accept message to a Request message, just before sending the PS_RDY message.

fields.name: Protocol Error
fields.description: An unexpected message was received from the partner device.

fields.name: Missing Get Capabilities Message Error
fields.description: The partner device did not respond to the Get_Sink_Cap or Get_Source_Cap message that was sent.

fields.name: Power Event Occurred Error
fields.description: An OVP, or ILIM event occurred on VBUS. Or a TSD event occurred.

fields.name: Can Provide Voltage or Current Later Error
fields.description: The USB PD Source can provide acceptable voltage and current, but not at the present time. A 'wait' message was sent or received.

fields.name: Cannot Provide Voltage or Current Error
fields.description: The USB PD Source cannot provide an acceptable voltage and/or current. A Reject message was sent to the Sink or a Capability Mismatch was received from the Sink.

fields.name: Device Incompatible Error
fields.description: When set to 1, a USB PD device with an incompatible specification version was connected. Or the partner device is not USB PD capable.

fields.name: CMD1 Complete
fields.description: Set whenever a non-zero value in CMD1 register is set to zero or ! CMD.

fields.name: PD Status Updated
fields.description: Set whenever contents of PD_STATUS register (0x40) change.

fields.name: Status Updated
fields.description: Set whenever contents of STATUS register (0x1A) change.

fields.name: Power Status Updated
fields.description: Set whenever contents of POWER_STATUS register (0x3F) change.

fields.name: Power Path Switch Changed
fields.description: Set whenever contents of POWER_PATH_STATUS register (0x26)  changes.

fields.name: USB Host No Longer Present
fields.description: Set when STATUS.UsbHostPresent transitions to anything other than 11b.

fields.name: Data Swap Requested
fields.description: A DR swap was requested by the Port Partner.

fields.name: Power Swap Requested
fields.description: A PR swap was requested by the Port Partner.

fields.name: Source Capabilities Message Received
fields.description: This is asserted when a Source Capabilities message is received from the Port Partner.

fields.name: New Contract as Provider
fields.description: An RDO from the far-end device has been accepted and the PD Controller is a Source. This is asserted after the PS_RDY message has been sent. See ACTIVE_CONTRACT_PDO register (0x34) and ACTIVE_CONTRACT_RDO register (0x35) for details.

fields.name: New Contract as Consumer
fields.description: Far-end source has accepted an RDO sent by the PD Controller as a Sink. See ACTIVE_CONTRACT_PDO register (0x34) and ACTIVE_CONTRACT_RDO register (0x35) for details.

fields.name: Overcurrent
fields.description: Set whenever an Overcurrent field (VBUS or VCONN) in the POWER_PATH_STATUS register (0x26) changes.

fields.name: Data Swap Complete
fields.description: A Data Role swap has completed. See STATUS register (0x1A) and PD_STATUS register (0x40) for port state.

fields.name: Power Swap Complete
fields.description: A Power role swap has completed. See STATUS register (0x1A) and PD_STATUS register (0x40) for port state.

fields.name: Plug Insert or Removal
fields.description: USB Plug Status has Changed. See Status register for more plug details.

fields.name: PD Hardreset
fields.description: A PD Hard Reset has been performed. See PD_STATUS.HardResetDetails for more information.

registers.name: Interrupt Mask for I2C1
registers.description: Interrupt mask bit field for INT_EVENT1. A bit in INT_EVENT1 cannot be set if it is cleared in this register. Bytes 1 to 10 of this register are port-specific, but Byte 11(

fields.name: Can Provide Voltage or Current Later Error
fields.description: Can Provide Voltage or Current Later Error

fields.name: Cannot Provide Voltage or Current Error
fields.description: Cannot Provide Voltage or Current Error

registers.name: Interrupt Clear for I2C1
registers.description: Interrupt clear bit field for INT_EVENT1. Writing 1 to a specific bit will clear that specific event in INT_EVENT1.

fields.name: Can Provide Voltage or Current Later Error
fields.description: Can Provide Voltage or Current Later Error

fields.name: Cannot Provide Voltage or Current Error
fields.description: Cannot Provide Voltage or Current Error

registers.name: Status
registers.description: Status bit field for non-interrupt events. Table 3-10. Status Register Field Descriptions

fields.name: SOC Ack Timeout
fields.description: Indicates whether the attached SoC has responded timely. 0h = SoC has responded timely 1h = SoC has not responded timely

fields.name: BIST
fields.description: Indicates if a BIST procedure is in progress. 0h = No BIST in progress 1h = BIST in progress

fields.name: Acting as Legacy
fields.description: Indicates when PD Controller has gone into a mode where it is acting like a legacy (non PD) device. It can take approximately 10 seconds for the PD controller to determine that it is attached to a legacy source or sink. 0h = PD Controller is not in a legacy (non PD) mode 1h = PD Controller is acting like a legacy sink 2h = PD Controller is acting like a legacy source 3h = Acting as legacy sink due to dead-battery.

values.0h: PD Controller is not in a legacy (non PD) mode
values.1h: PD Controller is acting like a legacy sink
values.2h: PD Controller is acting like a legacy source
values.3h: Acting as legacy sink due to dead-battery.

fields.name: USB Host Present
fields.description: USB host attachment status. 0h = No host present 1h = Attached source is not data capable 2h = Attached source is not USB PD capable 3h = Host present

values.1h: Attached source is not data capable
values.2h: Attached source is not USB PD capable

fields.name: VBUS Status
fields.description: Indicates the present state of VBUS. 0h = At vSafe0V (less than 0.8V)  1h = At vSafe5V (4.75V to 5.5V)  2h = Within expected limits 3h = Not within any of the other specified ranges

values.3h: Not within any of the other specified ranges

fields.name: Data Role
fields.description: PD controller data role. This is only valid once there is a connection. 0h = Upward-facing port (UFP)  1h = Downward-facing port (DFP)

fields.name: Port Role
fields.description: Current state of PD Controller CCx terminations. This also indicates the PD Controller Power Role, once connected. This bit does not toggle during Unattached.* state transitions. 0h = PD Controller is in the Sink role 1h = PD Controller is Source (CCx pull-up active)

values.0h: PD Controller is in the Sink role
values.1h: PD Controller is Source (CCx pull-up active)

fields.name: Plug Orientation
fields.description: Plug orientation indicator. Indicates port orientation when known  (requires connection). 0h = Upside-up orientation (plug CC on CC1)  1h = Upside-down orientation (plug CC on CC2)

fields.name: Connection State
fields.description: Details of a connected plug. 0h = No connection 1h = Port is disabled 2h = Audio connection (Ra/Ra)  3h = Debug connection (Rd/Rd)  4h = No connection Ra detected (Ra but no Rd)  5h = Reserved (may be used for Rp/Rp Debug connection)  6h = Connection present no Ra detected 7h = Connection present Ra detected

values.4h: No connection Ra detected (Ra but no Rd)
values.5h: Reserved (may be used for Rp/Rp Debug connection)

fields.name: Plug Present
fields.description: Status of the plug 0h = No plug is connected 1h = A plug is connected

registers.name: Power Path Status
registers.description: Power Path Status. Table 3-11. Power Path Status Register Field Descriptions

fields.name: Power Source
fields.description: Indicates current PD Controller power source. NOTE: Since the Dead Battery flag forces PD Controller to be powered from VBUS,  only 10b is valid when this flag is set. Any other setting indicates that the Dead Battery flag is not set. 0h = Reserved 1h = PD Controller is powered from VIN_3V3 2h = PD Controller is powered from VBUS 3h = Reserved

values.1h: PD Controller is powered from VIN_3V3
values.2h: PD Controller is powered from VBUS

fields.name: PPCable1 Overcurrent
fields.description: PP_CABLE1 overcurrent indicator. Asserted if an overcurrent condition exists on PP_CABLE1 (VCONN).

fields.name: PP1 Overcurrent
fields.description: PP_5V1 overcurrent indicator. Asserted if an overcurrent conditions exists on PP1 switch (PP_5V1).

fields.name: PP3 Switch
fields.description: Indicates current state of PP3 (PP_EXT1). 0h = PP3 switch disabled 1h = PP3 switch currently disabled due to fault 2h = PP3 switch enabled (system output)  3h = PP3 switch enabled (system input)

values.1h: PP3 switch currently disabled due to fault

fields.name: PP1 Switch
fields.description: Indicates current state of PP1 switch (PP_5V1). 0h = PP1 switch disabled 1h = PP1 switch currently disabled due to fault 2h = PP1 switch enabled (system output)

values.1h: PP1 switch currently disabled due to fault

fields.name: PPCable1 Switch
fields.description: Indicates current state of PP_CABLE1 switch. 0h = PP_CABLE1 switch disabled 1h = PP_CABLE1 switch currently disabled 2h = PP_CABLE1 switch CC1 enabled (system output)  3h = PP_CABLE1 switch CC2 enabled (system output)

registers.name: Port Configuration
registers.description: Configuration for port-specific hardware. This register configures hardware that is specific for each port and in most cases will not change in normal operation or will not require immediate action if changed. Any modifications to this register will cause a port disconnect and reconnect with the new settings. Initialized by Application Customization. Table 3-12. Port Configuration Register Field Descriptions

fields.name: VBUS Sink UVP Trip HV
fields.description: VBUS disconnect when power role is sink. The disconnect threshold is set to (1-VBUS_SinkUvpTripHV)*(min expected VBUS). The 000b setting follows the USB-C specification requirements. Use a non- zero value for additional margin. 0h = 5%  1h = 10%  2h = 15%  3h = 20%  4h = 25%  5h = 30%  6h = 40%  7h = 50%

fields.name: OVP for PP5V
fields.description: VBUS OVP settings while sourcing from PP5V. This applies while sourcing through PP1 or PP2. See data-sheet for voltage range. 0h = Use setting 0: 5.25 V (typical)  1h = Use setting 1: 5.5 V (typical)  2h = Use setting 2: 5.8 V (typical)  3h = Use setting 3: 6.1 V (typical)

fields.name: VBUS OVP Usage
fields.description: OVP configuration settings. These two bits are used to select the OVP trip-point. The PD controller automatically computes the lowest threshold that does not overlap with the expected maximum voltage  (including maximum tolerance allowed by USB PD specification). The OVP trip-point will be set at the selected percentage of the computed threshold. 0h = 100%  1h = 105%  2h = 111%  3h = 114%

fields.name: Disable PD
fields.description: Assert this bit to disable USB PD.

fields.name: TypeC Support Options
fields.description: Configuration for optional features. This register controls whether optional Type-C state machine states are supported. NOTE: These states are mutually-exclusive and these options only exist when specific Type-C state machines are used. 0h = No Type-C optional states are supported 1h = Try.SRC state is supported as a DRP 3h = Reserved

values.0h: No Type-C optional states are supported
values.1h: Try.SRC state is supported as a DRP

fields.name: TypeC State machine
fields.description: Port Configuration. 0h = Sink state machine only 1h = Source state machine only 2h = DRP state machine 3h = Disabled

registers.name: Port Control
registers.description: Configuration bits affecting system policy. These bits may change during normal operation and are used for controlling the respective port. The PD Controller will not take immediate action upon writing. Changes made to this register will take effect the next time the appropriate policy is invoked. Initialized by Application Customization. Table 3-13. Port Control Register Field Descriptions

fields.name: Charger Detect Enable
fields.description: Configure the types of legacy chargers to detect. 0h = Do not detect any legacy chargers 1h = Detect BC 1.2 chargers 2h = Reserved do not use 3h = Detect BC 1.2 and proprietary legacy chargers

values.0h: Do not detect any legacy chargers
values.3h: Detect BC 1.2 and proprietary legacy chargers

fields.name: Charger Advertise Enable
fields.description: Configure the types of legacy chargers to emulate. 0h = Do not emulate any legacy charger 1h = BC 1.2 CDP only 2h = BC 1.2 DCP only 3h = Reserved 4h = Reserved 5h = DCP Auto 1 (2.7V and DCP)  6h = DCP Auto 2 (1.2V 2.7V and DCP)  7h = Reserved

values.0h: Do not emulate any legacy charger

fields.name: Resistor 15k Present
fields.description: Configure D+ and D- termination. Assert this bit if there is a 15kOhm pull-down on D+ and D- (USB2.0 Host Phy pull-downs enabled). This should not be used for DCP or DCP Auto modes. 0h = System does NOT have 15 kOhm pull-down 1h = System has 15 kOhm pull-down

values.0h: System does NOT have 15 kOhm pull-down

fields.name: Enable Current Monitor
fields.description: Assert this bit to enable the current monitor (peak and average) that are read from the ADC_RESULTS register. While asserted the PD controller will remain in the active power mode.

fields.name: Unconstrained Power
fields.description: External power configuration. This also sets the Unconstrained Power bit defined by USB PD. When this bit is changed from 1 to 0 the PD controller will not attempt a power role swap with the Port Partner. If a power role swap is desired the host should issue a  'SWSr' 4CC command. 0h = No external power 1h = External power present

fields.name: Initiate Swap to DFP
fields.description: Configure DR_Swap to DFP initiation. If this bit is asserted, the PD Controller automatically initiates and sends DR_Swap requests to the Port Partner when appropriate if presently operating as UFP.

fields.name: Process Swap to DFP
fields.description: Configure response to DR_Swap to DFP. If this bit is asserted,  the PD Controller will automatically accept a DR_Swap request to become a DFP. Otherwise, the PD Controller will reject such a request.

fields.name: Initiate Swap to UFP
fields.description: Configure DR_Swap to UFP initiation. If this bit is asserted, the PD Controller automatically initiates and sends DR_Swap requests to the Port Partner when appropriate if presently operating as DFP.

fields.name: Process Swap to UFP
fields.description: Configure response to DR_Swap to UFP. If this bit is asserted,  the PD Controller will automatically accept a DR_Swap request to become a UFP. Otherwise, the PD Controller will reject such a request.

fields.name: Initiate Swap to Source
fields.description: Configure PR_Swap to source initiation. If this bit is asserted, the PD Controller automatically initiates and sends PR_Swap requests to the Port Partner when appropriate if presently operating as Sink  (C/P).

fields.name: Process Swap to Source
fields.description: Configure response to PR_Swap to source. If this bit is asserted,  the PD Controller will automatically accept a PR_Swap request to become a Source. Otherwise, the PD Controller will reject such a request.

fields.name: Initiate Swap to Sink
fields.description: Configure PR_Swap to sink initiation. If this bit is asserted, the PD Controller automatically initiates and sends PR_Swap requests to the Port Partner when appropriate if presently operating as Source  (P/C).

fields.name: Process Swap to Sink
fields.description: Configure response to PR_Swap to sink. If this bit is asserted,  the PD Controller will automatically accept a PR_Swap request to become a Sink. Otherwise, the PD Controller will reject such a request.

fields.name: TypeC Current
fields.description: Type-C Current advertisement. This setting is ignored if a Source role is not enabled and active. This setting is also ignored during an explicit USB PD contract, where the Rp value is used for collision avoidance as required by the USB PD specification. Note that when PP5V is low, the FW will only use the default Type-C current regardless of the value in this field. 0h = USB Default Current 1h = 1.5 A 2h = 3.0 A 3h = Reserved

registers.name: Boot Flags
registers.description: Detailed status of boot process. This register provides details on PD Controller boot flags, Customer OTP configuration, and silicon revision Table 3-14. Boot Flags Register Field Descriptions

fields.name: Revision ID
fields.description: Revision ID for the PD controller.

fields.name: Patch Config Source
fields.description: Source of patch configuration. This field indicates the source of the configuration patch that has been successfully loaded. 0h = No configuration has been loaded 4h = Reserved 5h = A configuration has been loaded from EEPROM 6h = A configuration has been loaded from I2C 7h = Reserved

values.5h: A configuration has been loaded from EEPROM
values.6h: A configuration has been loaded from I2C

fields.name: System TSD
fields.description: System thermal shut-down indicator. This bit is asserted if the PD controller is rebooting after the system thermal sensor caused a reset.

fields.name: Region 1 CRC Fail
fields.description: Region 1 CRC status indicator. This bit is asserted when the CRC of data read from Region 1 of EEPROM memory failed.

fields.name: Region 0 CRC Fail
fields.description: Region 0 CRC status indicator. This bit is asserted when the CRC of data read from Region 0 of EEPROM memory failed.

fields.name: Patch Download Error
fields.description: Asserted when a patch download error occurs.

fields.name: Region 1 EEPROM Error
fields.description: Region 1 status indicator. This bit is asserted when an error occurred attempting to read Region 1 of EEPROM memory. A retry may have been successful.

fields.name: Region 0 EEPROM Error
fields.description: Region 0 status indicator. This bit is asserted when an error occurred attempting to read Region 0 of EEPROM memory. A retry may have been successful.

fields.name: Region 1 Invalid
fields.description: Region 1 header status indicator. This bit is asserted when Region 1 header of the EEPROM memory was invalid.

fields.name: Region 0 Invalid
fields.description: Region 0 header status indicator. This bit is asserted when Region 0 header of the EEPROM memory was invalid.

fields.name: Region 1
fields.description: Region 1 attempted indicator. This bit is asserted when Region 1 of the EEPROM memory was attempted.

fields.name: Region 0
fields.description: Region 0 attempted indicator. This bit is asserted when Region 0 of the EEPROM memory was attempted.

fields.name: I2C EEPROM Present
fields.description: EEPROM presence indicator. This bit is asserted when an EEPROM device was discovered during boot.

fields.name: Dead Battery Flag
fields.description: Dead Battery flag indicator. This bit is asserted when the PD Controller booted in dead-battery mode.

fields.name: Patch Header Error
fields.description: Asserted when a patch bundle header errors.

registers.name: Received Source Capabilities
registers.description: Received Source Capabilities. This register stores latest Source Capabilities message received over BMC. Table 3-15. Received Source Capabilities Register Field Descriptions

fields.name: Number Valid PDOs
fields.description: Number of valid PDOs in this register. Each PDO is 4 bytes. (max of 7)

registers.name: Received Sink Capabilities
registers.description: Received Sink Capabilities. This register stores latest Sink Capabilities message received over BMC. Table 3-16. Received Sink Capabilities Register Field Descriptions

fields.name: Number Valid PDOs
fields.description: Number of valid PDOs in this register. Each PDO is 4 bytes. (max of 7)

registers.name: Transmit Source Capabilities
registers.description: Source Capabilities for sending. This register stores PDOs and settings for outgoing Source Capabilities PD messages. Initialized by Application Customization. Table 3-17. Transmit Source Capabilities Register Field Descriptions

fields.name: Power Path for PDO 7
fields.description: Configures which PP to use for PDO7. Same format as PowerPathForPDO2.

fields.name: Power Path for PDO 6
fields.description: Configures which PP to use for PDO6. Same format as PowerPathForPDO2.

fields.name: Power Path for PDO 5
fields.description: Configures which PP to use for PDO5. Same format as PowerPathForPDO2.

fields.name: Power Path for PDO 4
fields.description: Configures which PP to use for PDO4. Same format as PowerPathForPDO2.

fields.name: Power Path for PDO 3
fields.description: Configures which PP to use for PDO3. Same format as PowerPathForPDO2.

fields.name: Power Path for PDO 2
fields.description: Configures which PP to use for PDO2. 0h = Reserved 1h = Reserved 2h = PP_EXT1 is used for this PDO

values.2h: PP_EXT1 is used for this PDO

fields.name: Power Path for PDO 1
fields.description: Configures which PP to use for PDO1. 0h = PP_5V1 is used for this PDO 2h = PP_EXT1 is used for this PDO

values.0h: PP_5V1 is used for this PDO
values.2h: PP_EXT1 is used for this PDO

fields.name: Number Valid PDOs
fields.description: Number of valid PDOs in this register. Each PDO is 4 bytes. (max of 7)

registers.name: Transmit Sink Capabilities
registers.description: Sink Capabilities for sending. This register stores PDOs for outgoing Sink Capabilities USB PD messages. Initialized by Application Customization. The PD controller transmits the contents of this register as a Sink_Capabilities message after receiving a Get_Sink_Cap message unless its configuration or USB PD rules require a different response in the context. Writes to this register have no immediate effect. The PD controller updates and uses this register each time it needs to send a Sink Capabilities message. Each PDO in this TX_SINK_CAPS register follows the definition from the USB PD specification. For more details on the meaning of each field refer to the USB PD specification. Table 3-18. Transmit Sink Capabilities Register Field Descriptions

registers.name: Active PDO Contract
registers.description: Power data object for active contract. This register stores PDO data for the current explicit USB PD contract, or all zeroes if no contract. Table 3-19. Active PDO Contract Register Field Descriptions

fields.name: First PDO Control Bits
fields.description: Contains bits 29:20 of the first PDO. It does not matter which PDO was selected, this field is always drawn from the first PDO.

fields.name: Active PDO
fields.description: Power data object. This field contains the contents of the PDO Requested by PD Controller as Sink and Accepted by Source, once it is Accepted by Source.

registers.name: Active RDO Contract
registers.description: Power data object for the active contract. This register stores the RDO of the current explicit USB PD contract, or all zeroes if no contract. Table 3-20. Active RDO Contract Register Field Descriptions

registers.name: Autonegotiate Sink
registers.description: Configuration for sink power negotiations. This register defines the voltage range between which the system can function properly, allowing the PD Controller to negotiate its own contracts. Initialized by Application Customization. Table 3-21. Autonegotiate Sink Register Field Descriptions

fields.name: PPS Output Voltage
fields.description: This is the desired output voltage in 20mV units. This is inserted as- is into the Request USB PD message. Note that some PD controllers are unable to turn on the gate-drivers if VBUS less than 3.8V, check the VBUS UVLO value in the data-sheet.

fields.name: PPS Operating Current
fields.description: Operation current in Sink PPS mode. This is the desired operating current in 50 mA units. This is inserted as-is into the Request USB PD message.

fields.name: PPS Disable Sink Upon Non APDO Contract
fields.description: Sink path handling during supply type transition. If this bit is asserted and the selected supply type is NOT a PPS APDO, then the sink path is disabled before sending the Request message. The host should only assert this bit after a PPS contract has been negotiated. This bit has no effect unless PPSEnableSinkMode is asserted.

fields.name: PPS Required Full Voltage Range
fields.description: Select only a source with full voltage range. If this bit is asserted, a PPS supply type is not selected unless the APDO.MinVoltage ≤ TX_SINK_CAPS.MinPpsVoltage,  APDO.MaxVoltage ≥ TX_SINK_CAPS.MaxPpsVoltage, and APDO.MaxCurrent ≥ TX_SINK_CAPS.MaxPpsCurrent. This bit has no effect unless PPSEnableSinkMode is asserted.

fields.name: PPS Source Operating Mode
fields.description: Selection for CV or CC mode. If this bit is set to 1, then the PD controller assumes the system is in constant voltage mode and sets the VBUS disconnect threshold accordingly. If this bit is set to 0, then the PD controller will assume the system is in current limit mode and it will lower the VBUS disconnect threshold accordingly.

fields.name: PPS Request Interval
fields.description: Sink PPS request interval. This field sets the frequency at which the PD controller will send a new request to the source even if the host has not made any change in the request. 0h = 8 seconds 1h = 4 seconds 2h = 2 seconds 3h = 1 second

fields.name: PPS Enable Sink Mode
fields.description: Enable Sink PPS mode. If this bit is asserted, then the PD controller will attempt to negotiate a PPS sink contract. PPS contracts are prioritized over any other supply type.

fields.name: Auto Neg Capabilities Mismatch Power
fields.description: Capabilities Mismatch Power Threshold. If the selected PDO offers less power than what is specified in this register, then the PD controller will assert the Capability Mismatch bit in its Request message unless NoCapabilityMismatch is set to 1. (250mW per LSB)

fields.name: Auto Neg Min Voltage
fields.description: Minimum voltage to request. During PD power contract negotiation,  the PD controller will only select voltages that are greater than or equal to the value specified in this field. Not used unless AutoComputeSinkMinVoltage=0. (50mV per LSB)

fields.name: Auto Neg Max Voltage
fields.description: Maximum voltage to request. During PD power contract negotiation,  the PD controller will only select voltages that are less than or equal to the value specified in this field. Not used unless AutoComputeSinkMinVoltage=0. (50mV per LSB)

fields.name: Auto Neg Sink Min Required Power
fields.description: Minimum operating power required by the Sink. The PD Controller will always attempt to receive this power level from the Source. (250mW per LSB) See description in AutoComputeSinkMinPower field

fields.name: Auto Disable Sink Upon Capability Mismatch
fields.description: Sink path and capability mismatch settings. If this bit is asserted,  then any time the implicit or explicit power contract would cause the Capability Mismatch bit to be set the PD controller will disable the sinking path. The 'SRDY' 4CC task can override and enable the sink path. However, if the contract changes after the 'SRDY' has completed, the PD controller will disable the sink path if the contract causes a capability mismatch. This bit should only be asserted if the NoCapabilityMismatch bit is set to 0.

fields.name: Auto Compute Sink Max Voltage
fields.description: Configuration for maximum voltage. The PD controller can automatically compute ANMaxVoltage, or allow the host to specify it. 0h = Provided by host 1h = Computed by PD controller

fields.name: Auto Compute Sink Min Voltage
fields.description: Configuration for minimum voltage. The PD controller can automatically compute ANMinVoltage, or allow the host to specify it. 0h = Provided by host 1h = Computed by PD controller

fields.name: No Capability Mismatch
fields.description: Configuration for capability mismatch in RDO. There are two conditions that will trigger a capability mismatch: • If the attached source does not offer a PDO whose power is greater or equal to the ANSinkCapMismatchPower field in this register. • PPS is enabled in this register and the attached source did not offer a PPS PDO that matches the requirements in TX_SINK_CAPS. If either condition is true, then the PD controller will assert the capability mismatch bit in its request unless this bit is asserted. 0h = Capability mismatch enabled 1h = Capability mismatch disabled.

fields.name: Auto Compute Sink Min Power
fields.description: Minimum power sink requires. The minimum sink power is the largest power reported in any valid PDO in the TX_SINK_CAPS (0x33). The power for a particular PDO from the TX_SINK_CAPS follows for each supply type: • Battery Supply: OperatingPower • Variable Supply: MaxVoltage*OperatingCurrent • Fixed Supply: Voltage*OperatingCurrent. However, if the TX_SINK_CAPS register includes Battery supply type PDO(s), then ANSinkMinRequiredPower = maximum OperatingPower in a Battery supply type PDO. 0h = Provided by host 1h = Computed by PD controller

fields.name: No USB Suspend
fields.description: Value used for the NoUSBSusp Flag in the RDO. This is as defined by USB PD.

fields.name: Auto Neg RDO Priority
fields.description: Configuration for tie-breaker in PDO selection. The PD controller will find the set of PDOs that fulfill the voltage requirements. From that set of PDOs it will pick the one with higher power. If two acceptable PDOs have the same power, Fixed Supply Type is preferred, and then Variable Supply has second preference. If two PDOs have the same power and the same type, then this bit determines which PDO is selected. 0h = Higher voltage 1h = Lower voltage

registers.name: Power Status
registers.description: Details about the power of the connection. This register reports status regarding the power of the connection. Table 3-22. Power Status Register Field Descriptions

fields.name: Charger Advertise Status
fields.description: Charger Advertise Status 0h = Charger advertise disabled or not run 1h = Charger advertisement in process 2h = Charger advertisement complete 3h = Reserved

values.0h: Charger advertise disabled or not run

fields.name: Charger Detect Status
fields.description: 0h = Charger detection disabled or not run 1h = Charger detection in progress 2h = Charger detection complete none detected 3h = Charger detection complete SDP detected 4h = Charger detection complete BC 1.2 CDP detected 5h = Charger detection complete BC 1.2 DCP detected 6h = Charger detection complete Divider1 DCP detected 7h = Charger detection complete Divider2 DCP detected 8h = Charger detection complete Divider3 DCP detected 9h = Charger detection complete 1.2V DCP detected

values.0h: Charger detection disabled or not run
values.4h: Charger detection complete BC 1.2 CDP detected
values.5h: Charger detection complete BC 1.2 DCP detected
values.6h: Charger detection complete Divider1 DCP detected
values.7h: Charger detection complete Divider2 DCP detected
values.8h: Charger detection complete Divider3 DCP detected

fields.name: TypeC Current
fields.description: This field is redundant with PD_STATUS.CCPullUp in register 0x40 when SourceSink is 1b. This field is redundant with PORT_CONTROL.TypeCCurrent in register 0x29 when SourceSink is 0b. In the future, this redundant field may be removed. This field is intended for Type-C Sink operation. If the port is connected as source, the field is updated upon initial connection only. 0h = USB Default Current 1h = 1.5 A 2h = 3.0 A 3h = Explicit PD contract sets current

fields.name: SourceSink
fields.description: Source / Sink indicator. This bit is equivalent to PresentPDRole in register 0x40. It is replicated in this register for convenience. In the future, this redundant bit may be removed. 0h = Connection requests power 1h = Connection provides power (PD Controller as sink)

values.1h: Connection provides power (PD Controller as sink)

fields.name: Power Connection
fields.description: Asserted if there is a connection. This bit is asserted when PlugPresent is TRUE and ConnState is greater than 5h. So it is redundant with information from register 0x1A. It is replicated in this register for convenience. In the future this redundant bit may be removed. 0h = No connection 1h = Connection present

registers.name: PD Status
registers.description: Status of PD and Type-C state-machine. This register contains details regarding the status of PD messages and the Type-C state machine. Table 3-23. PD Status Register Field Descriptions

fields.name: Hard Reset Details
fields.description: Reason for Hard Reset 0h = Reset value no hard reset 1h = Received from Port Partner 2h = Requested by host 3h = Invalid DR_Swap request during Active Mode 4h = DischargeFailed. 5h = NoResponseTimeOut. 6h = SendSoftReset. 7h = Sink_SelectCapability. 8h = Sink_TransitionSink. 9h = Sink_WaitForCapabilities. Ah = SoftReset. Bh = SourceOnTimeout. Ch = Source_CapabilityResponse. Dh = Source_SendCapabilities. Eh = SourcingFault. Fh = UnableToSource. 10h = FRS failure 11h = Unexpected message 12h = Failure to to complete the VCONN recovery sequence within 200ms after PP5V rising edge

values.3h: Invalid DR_Swap request during Active Mode
values.12h: Failure to to complete the VCONN recovery sequence within

fields.name: Soft Reset Details
fields.description: Reason for Soft Reset 0h = Reset value no soft reset 1h = Soft reset received from Port Partner 2h = Reserved 3h = Reserved 4h = Received source capabilities message was invalid 5h = Message retries were exhausted 6h = Received an accept message unexpectedly 7h = Received a control message unexpectedly 8h = Received a GetSinkCap message unexpectedly 9h = Received a GetSourceCap message unexpectedly Ah = Received a GotoMin message unexpectedly Bh = Received a PS_RDY message unexpectedly Ch = Received a Ping message unexpectedly Dh = Received a Reject message unexpectedly Eh = Received a Request message unexpectedly Fh = Received a Sink Capabilities message unexpectedly 10h = Received Source Capabilities message unexpected 11h = Received a Swap message unexpectedly 12h = Received a Wait Capabilities message unexpectedly 13h = Received an unknown control message 14h = Received an unknown data message 15h = To initialize SOP' controller in plug 16h = To initialize SOP'' controller in plug 17h = Received an Extended message unexpectedly 18h = Received an unknown Extended message 19h = Received a data message unexpectedly 1Ah = Received a Not Supported message unexpectedly 1Bh = Received a Get_Status message unexpectedly

values.1h: Soft reset received from Port Partner
values.4h: Received source capabilities message was invalid
values.Fh: Received a Sink Capabilities message unexpectedly
values.12h: Received a Wait Capabilities message unexpectedly
values.15h: To initialize SOP' controller in plug
values.16h: To initialize SOP'' controller in plug
values.1Ah: Received a Not Supported message unexpectedly

fields.name: Present PD Role
fields.description: Present PD power role. The PD Controller is acting under this PD power role. 0h = Sink 1h = Source

fields.name: Port Type
fields.description: Present Type-C power role. The PD Controller is acting under this Type-C power role. 0h = Sink/Source 1h = Sink 2h = Source 3h = Source/Sink

fields.name: CC Pullup
fields.description: CC Pull-up value. The pull-up value detected by PD Controller when in CC Pull-down mode. 0h = Not in CC pull-down mode / no CC pull-up detected 1h = USB Default current 2h = 1.5 A (SinkTxNG)  3h = 3.0 A (SinkTxOK)

values.0h: Not in CC pull-down mode / no CC pull-up detected

registers.name: IO Config
registers.description: Application-specific GPIO Configurations. This register cannot be modified at run-time, the GPIO configurations are set according to the configuration during the boot process. Table 3-24. IO Config Register Field Descriptions

fields.name: GPIO 12 Mapped Event
fields.description: Event table mapping for GPIO12. See GPIO Event table.

fields.name: GPIO 11 Mapped Event
fields.description: Event table mapping for GPIO11. See GPIO Event table.

fields.name: GPIO 10 Mapped Event
fields.description: Event table mapping for GPIO10. See GPIO Event table.

fields.name: GPIO 7 Mapped Event
fields.description: Event table mapping for GPIO7. See GPIO Event table.

fields.name: GPIO 6 Mapped Event
fields.description: Event table mapping for GPIO6. See GPIO Event table.

fields.name: GPIO 5 Mapped Event
fields.description: Event table mapping for GPIO5. See GPIO Event table.

fields.name: GPIO 4 Mapped Event
fields.description: Event table mapping for GPIO4. See GPIO Event table.

fields.name: GPIO 3 Mapped Event
fields.description: Event table mapping for GPIO3. See GPIO Event table.

fields.name: GPIO 2 Mapped Event
fields.description: Event table mapping for GPIO2. See GPIO Event table.

fields.name: GPIO 1 Mapped Event
fields.description: Event table mapping for GPIO1. See GPIO Event table.

fields.name: GPIO 0 Mapped Event
fields.description: Event table mapping for GPIO0. See GPIO Event table.

fields.name: GPIO Event Polarity
fields.description: Controls polarity of a selected output event for each GPIO. Assert the bit for a given GPIO to invert the polarity of the event mapped to it. This field has no impact for input GPIO Events.

fields.name: GPIO 5 Analog Input Control
fields.description: Assert when GPIO5 is used as an analog input. This must also be asserted when PORT_CONTROL.ChargerDetectEnable or ChargerAdvertiseEnable is non-zero.

fields.name: GPIO 4 Analog Input Control
fields.description: Assert when GPIO4 is used as an analog input. This must also be asserted when PORT_CONTROL.ChargerDetectEnable or ChargerAdvertiseEnable is non-zero.

fields.name: GPIO AI Enable GPIO 2
fields.description: Assert when GPIO4 is used as an analog input.

fields.name: GPIO AI Enable GPIO 0
fields.description: Assert when GPIO4 is used as an analog input.

fields.name: Internal Pull Up Enable
fields.description: Controls weak pull-up setting for each configurable GPIO  (1=Enabled, 0=Disabled).

fields.name: Internal Pull Down Enable
fields.description: Controls weak pull-down setting for each configurable GPIO  (1=Enabled, 0=Disabled).

fields.name: Open Drain Output Enable
fields.description: Controls push-pull (0) vs. open-drain (1) setting for each configurable GPIO.

fields.name: Initial Value
fields.description: Controls default output level for each GPIO enabled as output  (0=Drive Low, 1=Drive High)

fields.name: GPIO Interrupt Enable
fields.description: Controls interrupt enable for each GPIO (1=Interrupt Enabled,  0=Interrupt Disabled). Note that all GPIO pins may not be configured as inputs (see the data-sheet).

fields.name: GPIO Output Enable
fields.description: Controls output enable for each GPIO (1=Output Enabled, 0=Hi-Z). Note that all GPIO may not be configurable as an output (see data- sheet).

registers.name: Type C State
registers.description: Contains current status of both CCn pins. Table 3-25. Type C State Register Field Descriptions

fields.name: TypeC Port State
fields.description: Present state of Type-C state-machine. 0h = Disabled 5h = ErrorRecovery 24h = Unattached.Accessory 2Bh = AttachWait.Accessory 45h = Try.SRC 4Eh = TryWait.SNK 4Fh = Try.SNK 50h = TryWait.SRC 60h = Attached.SRC 61h = Attached.SNK 62h = AudioAccessory 63h = DebugAccessory 64h = AttachWait.SRC 65h = AttachWait.SNK 66h = Unattached.SNK 67h = Unattached.SRC

fields.name: CC2 Pin State
fields.description: State of CC2 pin 0h = Not connected 1h = Ra detected (Source only)  2h = Rd detected (Source only)  3h = USB Default Advertisement detected (SInk only)  4h = 1.5A Advertisement detected (Sink Only)  5h = 3.0A Advertisement detected (Sink Only)

fields.name: CC1 Pin State
fields.description: State of CC1 pin 0h = Not connected 1h = Ra detected (Source only)  2h = Rd detected (Source only)  3h = USB Default Advertisement detected (SInk only)  4h = 1.5A Advertisement detected (Sink Only)  5h = 3.0A Advertisement detected (Sink Only)

fields.name: CC Pin for PD
fields.description: CC pin used for PD communication. 0h = Not connected 1h = CC1 is used for USB PD communication 2h = CC2 is used for USB PD communication

values.1h: CC1 is used for USB PD communication
values.2h: CC2 is used for USB PD communication

registers.name: ADC Results
registers.description: Provides access to measurements from the internal ADC. The PD controller periodically measures the pins mentioned in this register and updates the register accordingly. The frequency of the update depends upon the mode of the PD controller. For example, in Unconnected Sleep the PD controller will not update these registers. Table 3-26. ADC Results Register Field Descriptions

fields.name: IVBUS1_Mean
fields.description: Most recent current peak estimate through PP_5V1. If PORT_CONTROL.EnableCurrentMonitor = 1, this field is an estimate of the recent mean current. It is cleared upon attach for a new connection.(16.5mA per LSB)

fields.name: GPIO2
fields.description: Most recent voltage on the GPIO2 pin. (14mV per LSB)

fields.name: GPIO0
fields.description: Most recent voltage on the GPIO0 pin. (14mV per LSB)

fields.name: GPIO5
fields.description: Most recent voltage on the GPIO5 pin. (14mV per LSB)

fields.name: GPIO4
fields.description: Most recent voltage on the GPIO4 pin. (14mV per LSB)

fields.name: IVBUS1
fields.description: Most recent current measurement through PP_5V1. (16.5mA per LSB)

fields.name: VBUS1
fields.description: Most recent voltage on the PA_VBUS pin. (98mV per LSB)

fields.name: LDO3V3
fields.description: Most recent voltage on the LDO_3V3 pin. (14mV per LSB)

fields.name: ADCIN2
fields.description: Most recent voltage on the ADCIN2 pin. (14mV per LSB)

fields.name: ADCIN1
fields.description: Most recent voltage on the ADCIN1 pin. (14mV per LSB)

registers.name: Sleep Control
registers.description: Sleep configurations. Table 3-27. Sleep Control Register Field Descriptions

fields.name: Sleep Time
fields.description: Minimum time the PD controller waits before entering sleep mode. 0h = Reserved 1h = 100 ms 2h = 1200 ms 3h = Reserved

fields.name: Sleep Mode Allowed
fields.description: If this bit is asserted the PD controller will enter sleep modes after device is idle for Sleep Time.

registers.name: GPIO Status
registers.description: Captures status and settings of all GPIO pins. Check the device-specific datasheet for the available GPIO because it may vary by device type. Table 3-28. GPIO Status Register Field Descriptions

fields.name: GPIO 7 Direction
fields.description: This bit is asserted when this GPIO is configured as an output.

fields.name: GPIO 6 Direction
fields.description: This bit is asserted when this GPIO is configured as an output.

fields.name: GPIO 5 Direction
fields.description: This bit is asserted when this GPIO is configured as an output.

fields.name: GPIO 4 Direction
fields.description: This bit is asserted when this GPIO is configured as an output.

fields.name: GPIO 3 Direction
fields.description: This bit is asserted when this GPIO is configured as an output.

fields.name: GPIO 2 Direction
fields.description: This bit is asserted when this GPIO is configured as an output.

fields.name: GPIO 1 Direction
fields.description: This bit is asserted when this GPIO is configured as an output.

fields.name: GPIO 0 Direction
fields.description: This bit is asserted when this GPIO is configured as an output.

fields.name: GPIO 12 Data
fields.description: Asserted if a logic high is detected on the GPIO.

fields.name: GPIO 7 Data
fields.description: Asserted if a logic high is detected on the GPIO.

fields.name: GPIO 6 Data
fields.description: Asserted if a logic high is detected on the GPIO.

fields.name: GPIO 5 Data
fields.description: Asserted if a logic high is detected on the GPIO.

fields.name: GPIO 4 Data
fields.description: Asserted if a logic high is detected on the GPIO.

fields.name: GPIO 3 Data
fields.description: Asserted if a logic high is detected on the GPIO.

fields.name: GPIO 2 Data
fields.description: Asserted if a logic high is detected on the GPIO.

fields.name: GPIO 1 Data
fields.description: Asserted if a logic high is detected on the GPIO.

fields.name: GPIO 0 Data
fields.description: Asserted if a logic high is detected on the GPIO.

registers.name: Liquid Detection Config
registers.description: Liquid Detection Configuration Table 3-29. Liquid Detection Config Register Field Descriptions

fields.name: Enable Liquid Detection
fields.description: Enables liquid detection on the SBU pins connected to the GPIO on the PD Controller. In order for this to function correctly the proper external liquid detection circuitry must be in place.

fields.name: Enable Corrosion Mitigation
fields.description: Enable corrosion mitigation. Corrosion mitigation will disconnect the port, disabled the port, and pull down CC pins.

fields.name: Sample Time in 10ms Liquid
fields.description: Sample Time in multiples of 10ms (10ms per LSB as ms)

fields.name: Sample Time in 10ms No Liquid
fields.description: Sample Time in multiples of 10ms (10ms per LSB as ms)

fields.name: High Threshold ADC Liquid
fields.description: High Threshold ADC Liquid (14mV per LSB as mV)

fields.name: Low Threshold ADC Liquid
fields.description: Low Threshold ADC Liquid (14mV per LSB as mV)

fields.name: High Threshold ADC No Liquid
fields.description: High Threshold ADC No Liquid, provides hysteresis for exit out of Liquid Detected. (14mV per LSB as mV)

fields.name: Low Threshold ADC No Liquid
fields.description: Low Threshold ADC No Liquid, provides hysteresis for exit out of Liquid Detected. (14mV per LSB as mV)

fields.name: Number of Samples
fields.description: Number of samples (must be 2N) to take average

fields.name: Sleep Time In Sec Liquid
fields.description: Sleep in multiples of 1s when liquid is detected (1000ms per LSB as ms)

fields.name: Sleep Time In Sec No Liquid
fields.description: Sleep in multiples of 1s when liquid is not detected. (1000ms per LSB as ms)
